"How the Najdi scholars refuted the extremists

In the name of Allah, the Most Merciful, the Bestower of Mercy.

From: Abd al-Laf ibn Abd al-Ramn ibn asan
To: Abd al-Azz al-Khab

Peace be upon those who follow guidance, and upon the righteous servants of Allah.

To proceed:

I have read your letter and understood its contents, and what you intended by way of apology. However, you erred in your statement that what our shaykh, the father, denounced, namely your declaring the people of truth to be disbelievers, and your belief that you are correct, when you denied it.

You mentioned that your brothers from al-Naq are disputing with you and are arguing with you concerning us, and that they say that we have been silent regarding certain matters. And you know that they often mention this to attack our creed and to criticize our methodology. Even if they do not explicitly make Takfeer, they have approached its borders. So we seek refuge in Allah from misguidance after being upon guidance, from deviating away from the path of rectitude, and from blindness.

I witnessed, in the year 64, two men resembling you who were among the renegades in al-As. They abandoned the Friday prayer and the congregational prayer, and declared the Muslims of that land to be disbelievers. Their argument was similar to yours. They said: The people of al-As sit with Ibn Fayrz and mix with him and others like him, those who did not reject the ght by disbelieving in it, nor explicitly declare his grandfather to be a disbeliever who rejected the call of Shaykh Muammad, and did not accept it, and instead opposed it.

They both said: Whoever does not explicitly declare him to be a disbeliever is himself a disbeliever in Allah and has not rejected the ght by disbelieving in it. And whoever sits with him is like him. And upon these two false and misguided premises, they built rulings implying clear apostasy, such that they even abandoned returning greetings of Salam.

Their matter was raised to me, so I summoned them, threatened them, and spoke harshly to them. At first, they claimed that they were upon the creed of Shaykh Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb, and that his letters were with them. So I clarified their errors and refuted their misconceptions with what I had in that gathering.

I informed them that Shaykh Muammad is free from this belief and this path, and that he does not declare anyone to be a Kaafir except for those matters that the Muslims have unanimously agreed upon that makes one a Kaafir, such as major shirk, or rejecting the verses of Allah and His messengers, or any of that, after the proof has been shown to him and is properly conveyed. Such as making Takfeer on the one who worships the righteous, prays to them alongside Allah, and for making them His rivals in what He alone deserves in worship and authority over the creation.

This is agreed upon by the people of knowledge and faith. Every group among the followers of the various upheld Mazhabs has devoted a major chapter to this issue, mentioning its rulings, and that which causes apostasy and its implications, and explicitly mentioning Shirk. Ibn ajar even singled out this issue in a book he titled: al-Ilm bi-Qawi al-Islm (Informing about the Nullifiers of Islam).

These two Persians later showed repentance and remorse and claimed that the truth had become clear to them. Then they went to the coast and returned to that same doctrine. It reached us that they declared the leaders of the Muslims to be Kaafirs for communicating with the Egyptian rulers. Rather, they even declared as disbelievers those among the Muslim scholars who associated with those who communicated with them. We seek refuge in Allah from misguidance after being upon guidance and from relapse after rectitude.

We have also been informed about similar matters regarding you. And that you ....

"The Difference Between Dr al-Islm and Dr al-Kufr:

Ab Ysuf, the companion of Ab anfah, said:

A land is considered Dr al-Islm when the rulings of Islam are manifest in it, even if most of its inhabitants are disbelievers. And a land is considered Dr al-Kufr when the rulings of disbelief are manifest in it, even if most of its inhabitants are Muslims.
[al-Mabs by al-Sarakhs 10/144]

Ibn Mufli al-anbal said:

Every land in which the rulings of the Muslims prevail is Dr al-Islm, and if the rulings of disbelief prevail in it, then it is Dr al-Kufr. There is no third category beyond these two.
[al-db al-Shariyyah 1/213]

Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah said:

The majority said: Dr al-Islm is that land in which the Muslims reside and upon which the rulings of Islam are implemented. Whatever the rulings of Islam are not implemented upon is not Dr al-Islm, even if it is adjacent to it.
[Akm Ahl al-Dhimmah 1/266]

Al-Q Ab Yal al-Farr said:

Every land in which the dominance is for the rulings of disbelief over the rulings of Islam is Dr al-Kufr.
[al-Mutamad f Ul al-Dn, p. 276]

Al-Ksn said:

When the rulings of disbelief become manifest in a land, it becomes Dr al-Kufr; a land becomes Dr al-Kufr by the manifestation of the rulings of disbelief.
[Badi al-ani 9/4375]

Muammad ibn al-asan al-Shaybn said:

Every land is attributed either to Islam or to disbelief. A land is attributed to Islam when its rulings are implemented in it, and it is attributed to disbelief when its rulings are implemented in it. Just as one says Paradise is the abode of peace and Hell is the abode of ruin, due to the presence of peace in Paradise and ruin in Hell. Likewise, the manifestation of Islam or disbelief is by the manifestation of their rulings.
[Badi al-ani 9/4375]

Manr al-Buht said:

Hijrah is obligatory upon one who is unable to manifest his religion in Dr al-arb, which is the land where the rulings of disbelief prevail.
[Kashshf al-Qin 3/43]

Al-Allmah amad ibn Atq said:

Whoever has familiarity with what the verifying scholars have established knows that when shirk becomes manifest in a land, prohibited matters are openly practiced, and the symbols of the religion are abolished, then it becomes a land of disbelief, its peoples wealth may be taken and their blood made lawful. The people of this land have gone further by openly insulting Allah and His religion, and they have established laws which they enforce upon the people, in opposition to the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of His Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam). And you know that this alone is sufficient to expel the one who does it from Islam.
[al-Durar al-Saniyyah 9/257]

The Mufti Muammad ibn Ibrhm l al-Shaykh said:

A land in which judgment is made by man-made law is not a land of Islam, and migration from it is obligatory. Likewise, if idoltary becomes manifest without being condemned or changed, then migration is obligatory. Disbelief, by its widespread prevalence and open manifestation, renders it a land of disbelief.
[Fatw Muammad ibn Ibrhm 6/188]"

"Is ghusl sufficient in place of wu? (Shfii Fiqh).

"Do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. 

Shaykh Khalid al-Fulayj (may Allah free him) said in his explanation of as Sunah of al Barbahari:

Islam cannot be firmly established except upon complete submission to the texts of the Book and the Sunnah. So do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. 

Knowledge is not based on the minds and views of men; rather, it is with Allah and with His Messenger. So do not follow anything based on your desires, lest you deviate from the religion and exit Islam, then you would have no proof.

The Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) has clarified the Sunnah for his Ummah and made it clear to his companions. They are the Jamaat, and they are the great majority, and the great majority is the truth and its people. So whoever opposes the companions of Muhammad (salallahu alayhi wa salam) in anything of the Deen has disbelieved.

From this issue, an important principle is established: the texts of the Book and the Sunnah are not to be opposed by opinions, desires, tastes, spiritual feelings, or intellects. Rather, the stance of a Muslim regarding revelation is submission, belief, listening, obedience, following, and compliance, without disputing.

Then some ignorant people may come and narrate a hadith in Sahih al-Bukhari from Abu Hurairah in which the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) said:

If a fly falls into the vessel of one of you, let him immerse it, for on one of its wings is disease and on the other is cure.

Then the pretentious, the arrogant, the pseudo-intellectual, and the ignorant say: This contradicts reason. This is something impossible, how can an insect have disease in one wing and cure in the other?

We say: We believe in Allah, we believe in His Messenger (salallahu alayhi wa salam), we affirm, we hear, and we obey. Our Messenger does not speak from his own desire, rather, it is nothing but revelation revealed. So just as the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) informed us, we say: we hear and we obey.

As for you, by opposing the report of the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam):

If you know the report is authentic, then you have denied the Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) and committed disbelief by rejecting it.

And if you are ignorant, we teach you and establish the proof against you. If you persist upon your falsehood and misguidance, then you disbelieve in Allah the Most Great, because you have described the Messenger as a liar and claimed that his statement is not true.

But the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) does not speak from his own desire, rather, he speaks only revelation that is revealed.

So this is what is obligatory upon the Muslim: belief, listening, and obedience."

1.Rights of Allah (primary example: prayer)
2.Rights of servants (primary example: sanctity of life)

In the barzakh (the intermediate phase between death and Judgment), people are are not judged for the major sins themselves, but for their preludes and causes of lacking in them:

The prelude to prayer is to purify oneself of impurity and filth

" Jummah Returns to Masjid al-Aqsa But At What Cost?

Friday 10 April 2026

After Masjid al-Aqsa was closed for 40 days by decision of the occupation, after the people were deprived of it for five consecutive Jummahs, after they were prevented from praying there throughout the last ten nights of Ramadan and even on Eid al-Fitr, despite thousands striving to reach its gates and facing assault and arrest, it was eventually opened and the people returned.

The number of worshippers who travelled to Masjid al-Aqsa on this day had not been seen since last Ramadan. The Jerusalem Waqf estimated around 100,000 worshippers.

But notice the reality of this Jummah. The Israeli occupation continues to strip away whatever little remains of the role of the Jordanian Waqf within Masjid al-Aqsa, placing its police as a de facto authority over one of the most sacred places in Islam.

Heavily armed Israeli patrols were imposed across the entire Masjid. In the area outside the Dome of the Rock, in front of the Qibli Musalla, in the eastern areas, and in nearly every direction, intelligence units and armed border police moved freely, before the khutbah, during it, and even throughout the Jummah salah.

In 1967, following the Israeli occupation of East Jerusalem, salah returned to Masjid al-Aqsa on the understanding that all occupation forces would withdraw before the Adhan was called. That reality has been eroded slowly over time. These patrols first appeared on 13 March 2024 for the first time since the occupation began. Today, after the prolonged closure, they have expanded further, violating even the sanctity of the khutbah and the salah of Jummah itself.

Women in the area of the Dome of the Rock were forced to move repeatedly during the Khutbah by Israeli police. 

The constant presence of Israeli patrol units inside Masjid al-Aqsa acts as surveillance over the khateeb, ensuring that Gaza is not mentioned, that dua is not made for the oppressed, and that the aggression of the occupation is not spoken about from the minbar.

So yes, Masjid al-Aqsa has been opened, but it remains under ongoing attempts of Judaization, open in form, but controlled in reality. Its Islamic administration has been reduced to fragments, and what we are witnessing is the reshaping of authority over Masjid al-Aqsa.

Do not be deceived by the word opening.

The true opening of Masjid al-Aqsa is when it is returned fully to its people, free, honoured, and under no authority but that which preserves its sanctity.

Until then, your responsibility remains to hold firm to your dua. Strengthen your connection. Keep the cause of Masjid al-Aqsa alive in your homes, your conversations, and your communities. Make it a priority in your heart, and honour it with your presence whenever you are able.

"Regarding Pakistan / Afghanistan relations 

From my friend @LongshotIbrahim -- a brother who has written on and studied the subject considerably:

I have mentioned this before but in 1980s/1990s various Afghan factions, incl both Taliban and some of their foes, supported Pakistan and Kashmir and went out of the way to do so. This was while an increasingly pro-India US was running interference for India.

While Afg mujahideen in general backed Pakistan and Kashmir, outstanding case was Hikmatyar (Afg prime minister in 1993-94, then 1995-96), and his field commander Khalid Faruqi. Hizbul Mujahideen were trained and at least partly modelled on them for a decade or so.

Another major case was Arsala-Rahmani Daulat (prime minister 1994-95), who later joined Taliban and was murdered in 2012. A third was justice minister Jalaluddin Haqqani, father of today's Taliban interior minister Sirajuddin.

In general Taliban remained pro-Pakistan thru their first emirate. During the 1999 Pak war w India over Kashmir, Taliban prime minister Mohammad Rabbani offered to send thousands of fighters (but was declined bcos there wasnt really a manpower shortage).

At the end of 1999, Pak militants related to Kashmir jihad incl. Masood Azhar were released from Indian custody in a hostage exchange mediated, much to Pakistani glee, by Taliban Kandahar corps commander Akhtar Usmani.

Many of Kashmir-focused mujahideen also supported Taliban in late 1990s incl Saifullah Akhtar and AbdulJabbar Farooq. They fought directly under defence minister Ubaidullah Akhund and his lieutenant, today's Afghan security minister Ibrahim Sadar.

In east Afg, Taliban and Hizb and also Salafi networks were linked to Kashmir-focused mujahideen incl Salafi "Lashkar Taiba". One commander in Kunar who latee fought US at the famed Korengal valley, AbdulRahim, was a Hizbi turned Taliban but of Salafi persuasion, close to Taiba.

In 2001-02 after US invasion, Pakistan banned some of these groups (but tacitly tolerated others like Taiba). AbdulJabbar and Saifullah as well as Ilyas Kashmiri revolted against Pakistan, while Khalil and Azhar nervously walked a fine line.

In 2004, partly bcos of these rebels but also largely to distract US after Iran/Libya tattled, Pakistani army invaded Waziristan region. The army was surprised at local tribal backlash and adopted the line "Why are yoy fighting us, go and fight US in Afghanistan"

This line was supported by Afghan Taliban and practically every one of their leaders - emir Umar, Jalaluddin and Sirajuddin, Usmani, Daddullah Lang, Ubaidullah, chief justice NurMohammad Saqib, Mohammad Yasir, Sadar Azam - mediated on Pakistan's behalf with Waziristan insurgency.

However US intelligence (CIA) infiltrated Pakistan in 2005, using earthquake relief and humanitarian aid as a cover. Their targets were not only Taliban or Qaeda but also Pakistani groups linked to Kashmir, such as Taiba

Since the late 1990s US stance on Kashmir had been "Pakistan should forget Kashmir, make peace with India, and focus on its own Islamic radicalism", and this is what US (and their local lapdogs in Pakistan) pursued.

US also repeatedly sabotaged deals and ceasefires that, mediated by Taliban, army had made with insurgency in Waziristan. Ie it was US, not Taliban, who put Pakistan army and civilians at risk; it was US, not Taliban, who undermined Kashmir issue

Despite this Pakistan scrambled to appease and placate US, who would constantly bark "Do more" and undermine Pakistan further. It wasnt until 2011 that CIA agents were expelled as US/Pak relations hit a low and by then US was cracking down on Kashmir activists too

Meanwhile despite the Pak/US relations various Taliban networks incl Sirajuddin Haqqani continued to hit India; e.g. in 2008 Indian attache Ravi Mehta was killed at Kabul in a joint op w/ Taiba.

Sirajuddin was particularly hated by US for his role in a "triple agent" trick that killed CIA field commander Jennifer Matthews in what is still regarded as one of the most humiliating episodes in CIA history.

One CIA agent from this period - when agency was systematically destabilizing Pakistan, vilifying Kashmir, and bolstering India - was Sarah Adams, who has a particular visceral hatred for Sirajuddin Haqqani even by the agency's standards

Now bear in mind Adams' background. She was working for CIA in region at a time when it was actively involved in what even the most charitable appraisal could call anti-Pakistan/anti-Kashmir activity, during a period when Taliban were doing what Pakistan requested incl. v India.

however, after 2022 Pak coup - which was at least partly done for US approval - Pakistani pseudo-nationalists have embraced Adams as an unimpeachable source whenever she blames Taliban for anything, bcos they are eager to offload any responsibility onto "those ingrate Afghans"

Adams blames Taliban for everything, and she does this in large part bcos of her background and her resentment at Haqqanis, in particular. In 2023 she claimed that Sirajuddin had masterminded Hamas/Hezbollah war v Israel (oh, of course she also hates Palestine & loves Israel)

Adams doesn't give a shit abt Kashmir. She is pro-Israel, and Israel is pro-India, and India is anti-Kashmir. Her CIA career was spent partly in pro-India/anti-Kashmir activity. However, she knows that Pakistanis care about Kashmir and she wants to turn Pakistanis against Taliban

so after pro-US 2022 coup in Pakistan, Adams gets onto podcasts and "exposes" her target: "Oh, Taliban betrayed Kashmiris; oh, Taliban backstabbed Pakistan". If she could, she'd accuse Taliban of murdering Liaquat Ali-Khan in 1951 in order to whip up anti-Afghan sentiment in Pak

The fact is Taliban have a long record of supporting Pakistan and even Kashmir, and a looong record of opposing India at least on this issue. Whereas US,and its agents like Adams, have exactly the opposite record. But to justify current Pak war v Afg, she spreads these daft tales

Also, Taliban government is more or less the same ppl, with a handful of younger faces, as it was in 1996-2001 when they supported Pakistan. None of these people were anti-Pakistan until Pakistan, after the 2022 coup, started bombarding them."

"Salafis do not believe that the pagans of Makkah possessed a complete understanding of Tawheed al-Rububiyyah. When they assert that the pagans affirmed Tawheed al-Rububiyyah, they are referring to its foundational aspect (asl), which is the belief in one God as the ultimate creator and sustainer of the universe, the only being who is entirely independent. Salafis emphasize this point in response to saint venerators who argue that they are not guilty of shirk simply because they do not consider their saints to be independent of Allah. Salafis contend that the independence of a being is not the sole criterion for determining whether certain beliefs or actions constitute major shirk.

Salafis are well aware that the pagan Quraysh, while acknowledging Allah as the supreme creator, also attributed daughters to Him, worshipped idols, and failed to affirm His attributes correctly. These theological errors are considered distortions of Tawheed al-Rububiyyah.

Prominent Salafi scholars, such as Shaykh Abdulrahman Al-Barrak and Shaykh Yusuf Al-Ghafees, have argued that saint venerators also commit shirk in Rububiyyah by ascribing certain powers to their saints, powers that should be attributed exclusively to Allah.

To avoid semantic confusion, if Rububiyyah is primarily understood as Allahs independence and self-sufficiency, then Salafis assert that most of the pagan Quraysh acknowledged this foundational aspect. (However, Salafi scholars such as Shaykh Sultan al-Umayri, Abdulrazzaq Abu Badr, and Abu Bakr Muhammad Zakariyyah note that the Quraysh were not a homogeneous group and differed in their beliefs.) If Rububiyyah is defined in broader terms, while still rooted in the foundation of Allahs independence, Salafis maintain that the pagans only affirmed this foundational aspect.

The central issue, according to Salafis, is the affirmation of Allahs independence and the rejection of any justification by saint venerators that their actions do not constitute major shirk."

Muhammed Yutaev (Chechen:  ), also known as Magomed Yutaev, was a young Chechen fighter born in 1980 in Shalazhi, a village in Chechnya. He is remembered for his role in the First and Second Chechen Wars during his adolescence.

In early 1995, at the age of 14, Yutaev voluntarily joined the popular militia of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria to defend against the Russian invasion that began in December 1994. His commitment to the cause led to his selection for participation in significant operations, including Shamil Basayev's raid on Budyonnovsk in June 1995, a pivotal event in the war.

On May 21, 1996, Yutaev gave an interview to a Chechen presidential broadcaster, which brought him national recognition. Later that year, in August, he was involved in the Battle of Grozny, also known as Operation Jihad, which contributed to the partial liberation of the capital and was instrumental in securing victory in the First Chechen War.

At the end of 1999, during the onset of the Second Chechen War, Yutaev was captured by Russian forces. It is believed that he died due to torture suffered in Russian prisons . He is considered a martyr by Chechen separatists and was posthumously awarded the title "Qoman Turpal" (Hero of the Nation).

Yutaev's legacy endures as a symbol of youthful bravery in the Chechen resistance. His 1996 interview remains available online, serving as a testament to his dedication and the broader struggle for Chechen independence.

"A questioner asks: What is the ruling on offering congratulations and gifts on the occasion of Valentines Day, which falls in mid-February each year?

All praise is due to Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah and upon his family and companions. As for what follows:

This holiday is a festival of the kuffar and Mushrikeen. Even if it is called Valentines Day, it is a festival claimed by the Greeks, an ancient festival for a deity they worshipped besides Allah. 

Whether this holiday was for a deity they worshipped or merely for what they call and claim to be love, then congratulating others on it, celebrating this day, and honoring it is forbidden and not permissible.

We, the people of Islam, have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha. It is not permissible to congratulate others for any Eid besides these two. So congratulating others on this day and honoring it is not allowed; it is forbidden. You are not permitted to congratulate anyone on it, nor are you permitted to accept congratulations from anyone on this day. Rather, it is obligatory upon you and every Muslim to reject anyone who congratulates you on this day and to clarify to people that congratulating others on this day is forbidden and not permissible, and that it is a form of imitating the Kuffar and Mushrikeen in their false festivals, which are based on falsehood, disbelief, immorality, and wickedness.

How can they claim it is a day of love and magnify love while they are among the most hostile people toward Islam and Muslims, waging war against the religion of Allah and against the people of Islam? A Muslim should not be deceived by their labels or slogans. 

These Kuffar and wicked people are among the furthest from what is called love. They are the ones killing us, killing our brothers in Syria, Iraq, and everywhere, yet they claim this day is Valentines Day.

Therefore, it is not permissible to congratulate others on this day, nor is it permissible for a Muslim to honor it, give gifts on it, or accept gifts on it. It is a forbidden day; honoring it, celebrating it, and offering congratulations for such false festivals is forbidden.

We have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha, and these are the ones in which it is permissible to rejoice.

Likewise, commercial shops that sell gifts such as flowers, teddy bears, and similar items on this day, some people even change their pages or displays to red or use symbols associated with celebrating this day, selling flowers or anything that assists in honoring this day is not permissible. It is forbidden and is considered helping in falsehood. 

Allah says: Cooperate in righteousness and piety, and do not cooperate in sin and transgression.

Whoever sells such flowers with the intention of honoring that day, his sale is forbidden, and the money he earns from it is unlawful. Every body nourished by unlawful earnings, the Fire is more deserving of it. So it is not permissible to take such money, nor to sell flowers, change displays, alter slogans, or use symbols such as roses or the color red for this day, for these are forbidden and reprehensible matters.

It is obligatory upon a Muslim to honor the symbols of Allah, to honor the religion of Allah, to show disavowal of these Kuffar and wicked people, and to distinguish himself from them in their festivals and their false, misguided beliefs. For resemblance in outward matters leads to resemblance in inward matters.

We ask Allah for well-being and safety. 

Shaykh Khalid al Fulaij (may Allah free him)"
